2.2 Functional and presentation currency
Itemsincludedinthefinancialstatementsaremeasuredusingthecurrencyoftheprimaryeconomicenvironment
inwhichEthSwitchoperates (thefunctional currency). Allamounts areroundedtothenearest thousand,except
where otherwise stated. The financial statements are presented in Ethiopian Birr, in accordance with IAS 21 —
The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates and general presentation requirements under IFRS.
2.3 Going concern
The financial statements have been prepared on a going concern basis. Management has assessed EthSwitch’s
ability to continue as a going concern and has concluded that there are no material uncertainties that may cast
significantdoubtontheCompany’sabilitytocontinueoperatingforatleast12monthsfromthereportingdate,in
accordance with IAS 1 — Presentation of Financial Statements.
2.4 Basis of measurement
The financial statements have been prepared under the historical cost convention, except for the following:
(i) Financial assets and liabilities measured at amortized cost;
(ii) Derivative financial instruments measured at fair value; and
(iii) Non-derivative financial instruments with fixed or determinable payments that are not quoted in an active
market,whicharemeasuredatfairvaluethroughprofitorlossorfairvaluethroughothercomprehensiveincome
(OCI), in accordance with IFRS 9 — Financial Instruments.
The preparation of financial statements in accordance with IFRS requires the use of certain critical accounting
estimates and judgments by management in applying EthSwitch’s accounting policies. Areas involving a higher
degreeofjudgmentorcomplexity,orwhereassumptionsandestimatesaresignificanttothefinancialstatements,
are disclosed in the accompanying notes, in accordance with IAS 1 — Presentation of Financial Statements and
IAS 8 — Accounting Policies, Changes in Accounting Estimates and Errors.
2.5 Foreign currency translation
Foreigncurrencytransactionsaretranslatedintothefunctionalcurrencyusingtheexchangeratesprevailingatthe
dates of the transactions or at the dates of revaluation when items are remeasured. Foreign exchange gains and
losses arising from the settlement of such transactions, as well as from the translation of monetary assets and
liabilitiesdenominatedinforeigncurrenciesatthereportingdateexchangerates,arerecognizedinprofitorloss,
in accordance with IAS 21 — The Effects of Changes in Foreign Exchange Rates.
2.6 New standards, amendments and interpretations
Pronouncement and Effective Date
Effective 1 January 2026:
Updates to IFRS 9 and IFRS 7 refine the classification and derecognition of financial instruments, address ESG-
linked features, and expand disclosure obligations, annual improvements enhance the clarity and consistency of
IFRS 1, 7, 9, 10, and IAS 7, and additional guidance is introduced for contracts related to nature-dependent
electricity.

IFRS 18 supersedes IAS 1, establishing revised standards for financial statement presentation and disclosure, and

2.7 | Recognition | of revenue and expenses
Revenuefromservicesisrecognisedwhencontroloftheservicesistransferredtothecustomer,whichgenerally
occurs as the services are performed. For fixed-price service contracts, revenue is recognised over time by
reference to the stage of completion, measured based on the proportion of services provided to date relative to
the total contract value. Estimates of revenue, costs and progress toward completion are updated when
circumstanceschange.Anyrevisionstoestimates areaccountedforprospectivelyandrecognisedinprofit orloss
in the period in which the changes become known to management.
Consideration received in advance of providing services is recognised as a contract liability (deferred revenue)
until the related services are rendered. Where services are provided immediately, revenue is recognised at the
point of provision, which typically coincides with receipt of consideration. Deferred revenue is subsequently
recognised as revenue in profit or loss when the performance obligation is satisfied.
Other sources of income, such as commission, interest, and investment income, are recognised when it is
probable that economic benefits will flow to the entity and the amount of income can be measured reliably, in
accordance with the requirements of the relevant IFRS standards.
GrantsarerecognisedatfairvalueandtheGrantincomeisrecognizedonlywhenfundsareusedfortheprojector
meet donor conditions..
Expenses arerecognizedonanaccrual basis, regardless of the timingof cashpayments. Theyare recognizedin
the income statement when there is a reliable measurement of a decrease in future economic benefits related
either to a reduction in an asset or an increase in a liability, in line with IAS 1.
Expenses are measuredat historical cost. Expenses are also recognized inthe reportingperiod inwhich theyare
incurredifitisnotprobabletodirectlyassociatethemwithspecificincomeearnedduringthecurrentperiodand
when no future economic benefits are expected from those expenses.
Additionally,liabilitiesandrelatedexpensesarerecognizedwhenfutureoutflowsareprobableandcanbereliably
measured, according to IAS 37 – Provisions, Contingent Liabilities and Contingent Assets. |  |

2.10
Intangible assets acquired separately are measured on initial recognition at cost. Following initial recognition,
intangible assets are carried at cost less accumulated amortization and accumulated impairment losses, if any.
Internally generated intangible assets, excluding capitalized development costs, are not capitalized and
expenditure is recognized in the statement of profit or loss when it is incurred.
Theusefullivesofintangibleassetsareassessedaseitherfiniteorindefinite. Intangibleassets withfinitelivesare

2.11 Investment Property
EthSwitch measures investment properties using the cost model, recording them at cost less accumulated
depreciationandimpairmentlosses.Costincludesthepurchasepriceandrelatedexpensestopreparetheassetfor

useful life and residual value. Investment properties are tested for impairment annually or when needed, with
lossesrecognizedinprofitorloss.Gainsorlossesfromdisposalarealsorecordedinprofitorloss.Thisapproach

EthSwitch recognizes and measures income tax in accordance with IAS 12 – Income Taxes, which governs the

2.14 Employee benefits
i. Short-term benefits
The cost of short-term employee benefits (those payable within 12 months after the service is rendered, such as
paid annual leave and sick leave, bonuses and non-monetary benefits such as medical care) are recognized in the
period in which the service is rendered and are not discounted. The expected cost of compensated absences is
recognizedas anexpenseas theemployees render services that increase their entitlement or, in thecase of non-
accumulating absences, when the absence occurs. The expected cost of profit-sharing and bonus payments is
recognizedasanexpensewhenthereisalegalorconstructiveobligationtomakesuchpaymentsasaresultofpast
performance.
ii. Post employment benefit
Definedcontributionplansarepost-employmentbenefitplansunderwhichthecompanypaysfixedcontributions
intoseparateentitiesonamandatory,contractualorvoluntarybasis.Thecompanypaysfixedcontributions(11%
of the employee's basic salary) into state managed private employee’s state plan for individual employees. The
company has no further legal or constructive obligations to pay contributions in addition to its fixed
contributions,whicharerecognizedasanexpenseintheperiodthatrelatedemployeeservices,arereceived.The
company has no further payment obligations once the contributions have been paid.
The company is obliged by law to pay severance payment for eligible employees who served the company for
morethan5yearswhentheemploymentisterminated.Theamountpayableisonemonthfinalsalaryforthefirst

The liability is computed using Projected Unit Method to determine the present value of its defined benefit

(c) Classification of financial assets:-The classification is based on the business model for managing the
financial assets; and the contractual cash flow characteristics of the financial assets (i.e., whether cash flows
represent solely payments of principal and interest — SPPI test). This accounting policy is in accordance with
IFRS 9 — Financial Instruments.
Business model assessment for financial assets
EthSwitch classifies its financial assets based on the entity’s business model for managing those assets and the
contractual cash flow characteristics of the assets, in accordance with IFRS 9 – Financial Instruments.
The business model reflects how EthSwitch manages its financial assets to generate cash flows—whether solely
through collection of contractual cash flows, through both collecting contractual cash flows and selling financial
assets, or through trading or holding assets for short-term profit-taking (FVTPL).
For EthSwitch, the primary business model is to hold financial assets in order to collect contractual cash flows.
These assets typically include bank deposits, receivables from financial institutions, and short-term investments
suchas timedeposits. Theyareheldtomeet liquidityneeds andoperational funding,not fortradingorfrequent
sale.
Derecognition of Financial assets
Etswitch derecognizes a financial asset when the contractual rights to the cash flows from the asset expire, or
whenittransfersthoserightsinatransactionthattransferssubstantiallyalltherisksandrewardsofownership.If
EthSwitch neither transfers nor retains substantially all the risks and rewards and does not retain control of the
asset, the financial asset is also derecognized. This is in accordance with IFRS 9 — Financial Instruments.
Modifications of financial assets
AfinancialassetisderecognizedbyEthSwitchwhenitscontractualtermsaremodifiedandtheresultingcashflows
differ significantly from those of the original asset. In such cases, the original rights to the cash flows are
consideredtohaveexpired.Consequently,theoriginalfinancialassetisderecognized,andanewfinancialassetis
recognized at its fair value, including any applicable transaction costs. Fees arising from the modification are
included in the calculation of the gain or loss on derecognition or are treated as an adjustment to the carrying
amount of the new financial asset, depending on their nature. This treatment is in accordance with IFRS 9 —
Financial Instruments.
If the modification of a financial asset measured at amortized cost or at fair value through other comprehensive
income (FVOCI) does not result in derecognition, EthSwitch adjusts the gross carrying amount of the financial
asset to reflect any fees or costs incurred or received as part of the modification. The adjusted amount is
amortizedovertheremainingtermofthefinancialassetusingtheeffectiveinterestmethod.Thisisinaccordance
with IFRS 9 — Financial Instruments.
Impairment
Etswitch recognizes loss allowances for expected credit losses (ECL) on financial assets measured at amortized
cost or at fair value through other comprehensive income (FVOCI), including receivables such as bank fees
collections and advance payments, in accordance with IFRS 9 — Financial Instruments.

Financial liabilities classified as FVTPL include those held for trading or those designated at FVTPL upon initial

Derecognition of Financial liabilities
Etswitch derecognizes a financial liability when its contractual obligations are discharged, cancelled, or expire.
When a financial liability is replaced by another from the same lender on substantially different terms, or the
termsofanexistingliabilityaresubstantiallymodified,theoriginalliabilityisderecognizedandthenewliabilityis
recognized. Anydifference between the carryingamount of the liabilityderecognized andthe considerationpaid
(including any non-cash assets transferred or liabilities assumed) is recognized in profit or loss.
Modifications of financial liabilities
Etswitch derecognizes a financial liability when its contractual terms are modified and the modified cash flows
differsubstantiallyfromthoseoftheoriginalliability.Insuchcases, theoriginalfinancialliabilityisderecognized
and a new financial liability is recognized at fair value based on the modified terms. The difference between the
carrying amount of the derecognized liability and the consideration paid, including any non-financial assets
transferred and liabilities assumed, is recognized in profit or loss.
Ifthemodificationdoes not result inderecognition, theamortized cost of thefinancial liabilityis recalculatedby
discounting the modified cash flows using the original effective interest rate. Any resulting gain or loss is
recognizedinprofitorloss.ThisisinaccordancewithIFRS9FinancialInstruments,specificallytherequirements
on derecognition and modification of financial liabilities.
iii. Offsetting
Financial assets and liabilities are offsetand presentedas a net amountin thestatement of financial positiononly
when Etswitch has a legally enforceable right to set off the recognized amounts and intends either to settle on a
netbasisortorealizetheassetandsettletheliabilitysimultaneously.Incomeandexpensesarepresentedonanet
basis onlywhenpermittedunder IFRSor whenrelatedtogains andlossesarisingfromsimilar transactions. This
accounting treatment is in accordance with IAS 32 – Financial Instruments: Presentation, which governs the
offsetting of financial assets and liabilities.

 | Significant increase in credit risk |  | 
The Company assesses whether a significant increase in credit risk has occurred since initial recognition by

The company maintains a conservative policy regarding currency and interest rate risks and does not engage in
speculation in the markets. In addition, the company does not speculate or trade in derivative financial
instruments.

interest rates is low as it has no outstanding borrowings.

The company operates a defined benefit severance pay plan for its employees, as per the company's policy and the
relevant labor laws. The plan provides a lump-sum payment upon termination of employment, calculated as one
month's final basic salary for each completed year of service.
a) Valuation Method and Assumptions
The present value of the defined benefit obligation has been calculated using the Projected Unit Credit Method. This method
attributes the total benefit to the current period (service cost) and to prior periods (through the interest on the present
obligation).
The principal actuarial assumptions used were as follows:
The discount rate is based on the estimated market yield of high-quality corporate bonds in Ethiopia. The salary growth
assumption considers inflation, seniority, and promotion.
For the year ending 30 June 2025, the discount rate is assumed to remain at 15.0%, while the inflation rate is projected to
decrease to 12.2%. The salary increase rate is expected to follow a similar trend, also reducing to 12.2%. This indicates that
while the discount rate remains stable, both inflation and salary growth are anticipated to moderate in 2025.

37 Related party transaction
The Company is required to disclose transactions with related parties in accordance with IAS 24 ―Related Party
Disclosures.‖ Related parties include entities or individuals that have control, joint control, or significant influence
over the Company, as well as key management personnel and their close family members.
EthSwitch S.C. is a share company established by the National Bank of Ethiopia (NBE), commercial banks,
microfinanceinstitutions,andotherlicensedfinancialsectoroperators.Theseinstitutionsarebothshareholdersand
customers of the Company, as they utilize the interoperable payment system and related services operated by
EthSwitch. Accordingly, transactions with these entities are considered related party transactions.
The principal related parties of the Company comprise its shareholders, Board of Directors, key management
personnel, and close family members of such individuals. Related parties also include entities over which
shareholders and key management personnel exercise significant influence.
In the normal course of business, the Company generates a significant portion of its revenue from related parties.
Thismainlyconsists of servicefees collectedona monthlyandquarterlybasis fromshareholder banksandfinancial
institutions for switching, clearing, settlement, and other interoperable payment services. These transactions are
recurring in nature, arise from the Company’s primary operations, and are conducted at rates approved by the
Board of Directors and applied consistently across all participants.
Outstanding balances at the reporting date from related party transactions are included under receivables and
payablesinthefinancialstatements.Suchbalancesareunsecured,interest-free,andaresettledintheordinarycourse
of business. No impairment has been recognized against related party receivables during the reporting period.
Key management personnel compensation, including salaries, allowances, and other short-term employee benefits,
is separately disclosed in the notes to the financial statements. The Company did not provide loans, advances, or
guarantees to directors or key management personnel during the year.

38 ETHSWITCH SHARE COMPANY Note to the Financial Statements For the year end 30 June 2025 30 June 2025 30 June 2024 32 Termination benefit liabilities in Birr in Birr Accrued severance pay 1 0,258,902 4,836,861 10,258,902 4,836,861 The company operates a defined benefit severance pay plan for its employees, as per the company's policy and the relevant labor laws. The plan provides a lump-sum payment upon termination of employment, calculated as one month's final basic salary for each completed year of service. a) Valuation Method and Assumptions The present value of the defined benefit obligation has been calculated using the Projected Unit Credit Method. This method attributes the total benefit to the current period (service cost) and to prior periods (through the interest on the present obligation). The principal actuarial assumptions used were as follows: The discount rate is based on the estimated market yield of high-quality corporate bonds in Ethiopia. The salary growth assumption considers inflation, seniority, and promotion. For the year ending 30 June 2025, the discount rate is assumed to remain at 15.0%, while the inflation rate is projected to decrease to 12.2%. The salary increase rate is expected to follow a similar trend, also reducing to 12.2%. This indicates that while the discount rate remains stable, both inflation and salary growth are anticipated to moderate in 2025.

41 ETHSWITCH SHARE COMPANY Note to the Financial Statements For the year end 30 June 2025 37 Related party transaction The Company is required to disclose transactions with related parties in accordance with IAS 24 ―Related Party Disclosures.‖ Related parties include entities or individuals that have control, joint control, or significant influence over the Company, as well as key management personnel and their close family members. EthSwitch S.C. is a share company established by the National Bank of Ethiopia (NBE), commercial banks, microfinanceinstitutions,andotherlicensedfinancialsectoroperators.Theseinstitutionsarebothshareholdersand customers of the Company, as they utilize the interoperable payment system and related services operated by EthSwitch. Accordingly, transactions with these entities are considered related party transactions. The principal related parties of the Company comprise its shareholders, Board of Directors, key management personnel, and close family members of such individuals. Related parties also include entities over which shareholders and key management personnel exercise significant influence. In the normal course of business, the Company generates a significant portion of its revenue from related parties. Thismainlyconsists of servicefees collectedona monthlyandquarterlybasis fromshareholder banksandfinancial institutions for switching, clearing, settlement, and other interoperable payment services. These transactions are recurring in nature, arise from the Company’s primary operations, and are conducted at rates approved by the Board of Directors and applied consistently across all participants. Outstanding balances at the reporting date from related party transactions are included under receivables and payablesinthefinancialstatements.Suchbalancesareunsecured,interest-free,andaresettledintheordinarycourse of business. No impairment has been recognized against related party receivables during the reporting period.

Key management personnel compensation, including salaries, allowances, and other short-term employee benefits, is separately disclosed in the notes to the financial statements. The Company did not provide loans, advances, or guarantees to directors or key management personnel during the year. Compensation of key management personnel of the Company Key management personnel of the Company comprise the Chief Executive Officer, directors, and members of the Board of Directors.
